segments.text: $285 billion got wiped off software stocks in a single quarter and it's not because people stopped needing software It's
because they stopped needing to pay someone else to build it for them I used to pay over $1,000 a
month in software subscriptions GoHighLevel
Zapier Semrush the whole stack Now I pay $80 for Claude Code and I've replaced almost all of it And
I'm not an outlier This is happening across the entire industry SaaS no-code platforms even the AI coding tools themselves
they're all getting eaten alive $285 billion That's how much got wiped from software stocks in a single quarter Another
day of not a correction a structural collapse and most SaaS founders still think it's temporary It's not Enterprise SaaS
is structurally breaking Seat-based pricing made sense when you needed humans to operate software Salesforce charges per seat HubSpot charges
per seat ServiceNow charges per seat Entire business model assumes a human is sitting in each seat using the product
every day AI replaces the humans The seats are empty now Revenue collapses The entire model breaks when a user
disappears And nobody's coming back to fill those seats And that's just a squeeze from above There's a second squeeze
coming from below Solo builders are replacing entire SaaS products by building custom tools with Claude code not prototypes not
demos production tools that run real businesses Two directions both lethal The traditional SaaS companies getting crushed from both sides
at once But while enterprise SaaS crashes something weird is happening on the other side Micro SaaS is growing 30%
year over year Solo founders pulling $5,000 to $50,000 a month with 70% margins SaaS isn't dying it's being redistributed
Like music the industry didn't didn't shrink when Spotify killed the CD It shifted from labels to independent artists Same
thing is happening with software right now from companies with 500 employees to individuals with one API key That means
the opportunity isn't disappearing it's moving And if you're not paying attention to where it's going it's moving without you
I run a SaaS that does $100,000 a year in revenue and even my own product is vulnerable to this
exact shift Nobody is immune I know this because I've been on both sides I've built SaaS products I've sold
SaaS products I've watched SaaS eat entire industries for a decade and now I'm watching something eat SaaS itself The
macro data says it's structural but I've got something better than macro data I've got receipts Let me show you
exactly which subscriptions I killed and what replaced every single one of them Semrush $130 to $450 a month I
canceled it Zapier $22 to $70 a month canceled Midjourney Premiere Pro vidIQ GoHighLevel at $497 a month all canceled
Here's what replaced every single one of them 6 subscriptions $700
a month and the replacement cost for all 6 combined is $80 one Claude Code Max subscription So let me
walk you through each one Semrush the SEO platform everyone swears by Ranked keywords competitor analysis keyword gaps Google Trends
serious tools for serious marketers Most people assume you need a massive platform for real SEO data You don't I
only use 4 things ranked keywords competitor analysis keyword gaps and trends Semrush charges up to $450 a month for
a dashboard with hundreds of Features I never touch Sound familiar I built SEO Agent MCP It hits DataForSEO's
APIs directly Ranked keywords competitor analysis keyword gaps Google Trends The 4 things I actually need cost about $50 a
month for the API The MCP server itself is free No downgrade just no $400 dashboard wrapped around it Semrush
was the easy one The data was already available through an API The next one surprised me because I thought
automation tools were irreplaceable Zapier If you've never used it It's the glue between your apps Trigger fires action runs
done Clean and simple Connects your Stripe to your Slack sends a notification when someone pays Easy Until you need
actual logic conditional branches error handling retries multi-step workflows where step 4 depends on what step 2 returned That's when
Zapier gets expensive They charge per task and complex automations eat through those tasks fast You end up paying $50
$60 $70 a month just to move data between apps I replaced all of it with 19 Claude Code Agents
and 24 custom skills And I don't mean simple if this then that automations
Full client delivery pipelines that take in a brand run an audit across 3 search engines build a playbook and
generate a report Video production workflows that go from script to rendered in 4K video Audit to report chains that
run end to end without me touching them Cost beyond the Claude code subscription is zero Every workflow you're paying
Zapier to run is really just paying for a UI UI that limits what your automation can do When you
remove that layer your automations aren't bound by someone else's feature set anymore They can be as complex as your
business actually needs them to be So if those limits are frustrating you and you want to see how I
actually build these agent workflows I'm pushing builders through it inside SaaS Accelerator links in the description But the next
3 kills are where people push back hardest Midjourney $10 to $30 a month replace it with Imagen MCP running
on Gemini's API pennies per image batch generation 4K output You give it a prompt it gives 3 variations For
business imagery product shots slides thumbnails Gemini handles it And the cost difference isn't even close Premiere Pro this is
the one where people say you're crazy for replacing it The Creative Tool everyone assumes you need Adobe charges you
$20 to $45 a month for it But for YouTube content 90% of editing is repetitive— cuts layouts overlays text
zooms— the same patterns every single video You're paying a monthly subscription for a Swiss Army knife when all you
use is the blade And Adobe keeps raising the price while you keep doing the same 10 things Video editor
Remotion— open source 4K rendering 25+ layer paths programmatic So I define the edit once and it renders every time
Cost is zero VidIQ— $22.50 a month for trend data and keyword research They took every YouTuber installs first Replace
with Trend Scanner MCP Scans GitHub trending Hacker News and Reddit in parallel Catches trends at stage 1 before they
ever hit YouTube All free APIs $0 a month Those 5 were each $22 to $130 a month Annoying but
manageable The next one was $497 and killing it felt like jumping off a cliff GoHighLevel If you've never heard
of it it's an all-in-one platform for agencies CRM funnels email SMS scheduling automation Hundreds of features crammed into one
dashboard $497 a month every month like clockwork I used 4 features 4 out of hundreds paying $497 every single
month for a platform where 96% of the features were deadweight just sitting there doing nothing So I built exactly
what I needed with Claude Code Only the 4 features I actually use every day not the full CRM replacement Nobody
needs a full CRM replacement A surgical extraction of what matters Leaner faster does exactly what I tell it to
do Nothing more nothing less And it doesn't charge me $500 a month for the privilege of ignoring 96% of
it Now I know what some of you might be thinking Cool demos but these are probably toy replacements Simple
scripts pretending to be real tools They're not The SEO tool runs real competitor analysis on live production data The
same queries the same data sources the same results you'd get from SEMrush The video editor renders 4K video with
25+ layout patterns This video you're watching right now was edited with it The agent system handles full client delivery
pipelines end to end with no babysitting These aren't weekend projects I built to prove a point Here are tools
I use every single day to run a real business that does real revenue Total before $700 to $1,100 a
month Total after $80 That's $7,400 to $12,200 per year back in your pocket Not theoretical savings real money I
used to spend every single month that now stays in my bank account Every dollar you save on SaaS is
a dollar you don't need to earn from clients just to break even That changes your break-even math your pricing
power your run rate everything A business that costs $800 less per month to run is a a business that
survives 8 months longer on the same runway And replacements actually do more than the originals because they're built for
exactly how I work No features I never touch no UI standing between me and what I need to do
no monthly price creeping up every year because some board decided to hit their quarterly targets If this kill list
is making you rethink your own subscriptions hit like so more builders see this But there's a category of tools
I haven't mentioned yet The ones that claim to be on your side of this shift the ones you think
are helping you they might be the most vulnerable of all Cursor
Bolt Replit Agent they all have the same problem They're middlemen and middlemen always get squeezed Look at the pattern
Every single SaaS on the kill list had the exact same structure A company sitting between you and the underlying
capability charging you a markup for a user interface That's it That's the entire business model Take something that exists
wrap it in a dashboard and charge monthly Semrush sat between you and SEO data Zapier sat between you and
raw API connections Premiere Pro sat between you and FFmpeg which is free and open source GoHighLevel SaaS between you
and 4 basic features you could build in a weekend Now look at AI coding tools Cursor sits between you
and cloud Lovable sits between you and cloud Bolt sits between you and cloud Same pattern They add a UI
on top They charge you a markup and they control how many requests you get to make They're wrappers Same
exact structure as every SaaS on the kill list just with a different label and a fancier landing page And
to prove this is already happening landed in June 2025 Cursor the most popular AI coding tool in the world
cut their their Pro plan from 500 requests down to 250 Just like that cut in half The SEO had
to publicly apologize on Twitter Why did it happen The underlying API costs from Anthropic rose and Cursor absorbed the
hit They had no choice They couldn't raise prices without losing the users They couldn't eat the cost without losing
money So they cut what you get in half When you're a wrapper your economics are controlled by the company
you wrap You don't set your own margins They do Every wrapper-based tool has a ticking clock The moment the
model provider offers direct access at the same price point the wrapper's entire value proposition collapses overnight And this pattern
isn't new it's ancient Travel agents sat between you and airlines music labels sat between you and artists taxi companies
sat between you and the drivers Every single time the underlying capability becomes directly accessible the middleman gets squeezed out
No exceptions Claude Code talks directly to Anthropic API No wrapper no middleman no company between you and the capability
that can decide tomorrow to slash your requests in half So what's the move Stop asking which tool should I
use Start asking a different question What's the layer I'm paying for and can I remove it Every subscription is
a bet that the vendor can do something you can't That bet is expiring fast If you want to learn
which layers to remove first and how to build what replaces them SaaS Accelerator link is in the description You
just watched 6 subscriptions expire 6 bets that vendors could do something you can't 6 middlemen every single one lost
Knowing what to kill is half of it Knowing what to build first in what order and how to avoid
rebuilding the whole thing 3 times that's the harder part SaaS Accelerator I'm coaching builders through exactly this transition Links
in the description
